rulings.Case Title: STEVE RYAN VS. MITCHELL ROSENFELD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Steve Ryan Aka Stephen Ryan A Vexatious Litigant
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 21, 2022 line 1. DEFENDANT SILKE FINIS' NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO DEEM STEVE RYAN AKA STEPHEN RYAN A VEXATIOUS LITIGANT Hearing Required. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: EUGENE BARNES VS. EDWARD MILLER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 21, 2022 line 3. DEFENDANTS VINH CAO AND MAI CAO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is DENIED. Defendant does not meet their initial burden of showing that the cause of action for fraudulent concealment has no merit because Defendant does not establish that one or more elements of fraudulent concealment cannot be established. The authority cited by Defendant does not establish that Defendant had no duty to disclose the prior flooding events to Plaintiff. Further, Plaintiff shows there is a triable issue of fact on various elements of the cause of action, including whether the Plaintiff was aware of previous flooding events before signing the lease and whether Plaintiff would have acted differently had Plaintiff been aware. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
